concerns.text: Task T015 implements 'valid_logging_proportion' < 80% check but omits the explicit logic to detect 'any single data gap >20% of total session time' as required by FR-007 and US-1. The implementer cannot execute the full exclusion criteria without this specific gap-size logic.

Task T019 schedules the 'power analysis justification' in Phase 2 (Foundational). FR-008 and the plan explicitly state this justification must exist 'prior to initiating data collection' (Phase 3). The task ordering fails to enforce this temporal dependency, risking implementation of the pipeline before the sample size is justified.

FR-001 mandates aggregating continuous logs into three discrete categories (Low/Moderate/High) for 'descriptive analysis only'. The current tasks (T025-T027) focus solely on the continuous LMM and post-hoc tests. There is no concrete task to generate the required descriptive statistics table or summary report for these categories.

The spec's Edge Cases require handling 'completely silent environment (0dB)' by treating it as 'Low' noise and checking for model singularity. Task T016 only addresses RT outliers. The implementer lacks instructions to handle the 0dB edge case in the data ingestion or modeling logic.

SC-003 requires measuring the 'stability of the Moderate noise effect size' specifically. Task T035 only mentions reporting 'effect sizes' generally. The task lacks the concrete instruction to isolate and report the stability metric specifically for the 'Moderate' category, making verification of SC-003 impossible.

Task T014 implements the 'simulation' of the calibration protocol but lacks a distinct step to 'validate' the simulation logic against a reference standard or document the 'reference tone' parameters used. This makes the simulation's fidelity unverifiable.
